meter_status_voltmeter_open: Incorrect placement: circuit is nearly open
meter_guidance_ammeter_short: An ammeter has nearly zero resistance. Across parallel nodes it bypasses the load and draws extremely high current. Move it into the series path.
meter_guidance_voltmeter_open: A voltmeter has extremely high resistance. In series it nearly stops current. Connect it across the component whose voltage difference you want.
meter_guidance_ammeter_ok: The ammeter is in series, so circuit current passes through it.
meter_guidance_voltmeter_ok: The voltmeter is in parallel, so it compares electric potential across the branch while drawing negligible current.
intro_g_low: Build a circuit! Connect parts to make electricity flow and light up bulbs.
intro_g_mid: Build series and parallel circuits. Add resistors, bulbs, and switches. Watch how changing voltage affects current!
intro_g_high: Explore Ohm's Law (V=IR) by building circuits. Compare series vs parallel. Measure with ammeter and voltmeter.
intro_g_adv: Analyze DC circuits using Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws, and power dissipation. Series, parallel, and mixed configurations.
state_ready_note: Start with a load, then close the loop.
state_open_note: Close switches or add a real load.
state_current_flowing_note: Use the meters and readouts to test the model.
bench_headline: Build the loop, then prove the numbers
aria_bench_visual: Circuit bench visual showing battery, wire loop, load, and live meter readouts
svg_series_caption: Series: same current through every part
ammeter_rule_desc: Series connection; very low internal resistance.
voltmeter_rule_desc: Parallel connection; very high internal resistance.
